vulnerability.title: Set number of Password Hashing Rounds - system-auth
vulnerability.description: Configure the number or rounds for the password hashing algorithm. This can be accomplished by using the rounds option for the pam_unix PAM module. In file /etc/pam.d/system-auth append rounds= to the pam_unix.so entry, as shown below: password sufficient pam_unix.so ...existing_options... rounds= The system's default number of rounds is 5000.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( rpm --quiet -q pam && rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ); then var_password_pam_unix_rounds='' if [ -e "/etc/pam.d/system-auth" ] ; then PAM_FILE_PATH="/etc/pam.d/system-auth" if [ -f /usr/bin/authselect ]; then if ! authselect check; then echo " authselect integrity check failed. Remediation aborted! This remediation could not be applied because an authselect profile was not selected or the selected profile is not intact. It is not recommended to manually edit the PAM files when authselect tool is available. In cases where the default authselect profile does not cover a specific demand, a custom authselect profile is recommended." exit 1 fi CURRENT_PROFILE=$(authselect current -r | awk '{ print $1 }') # If not already in use, a custom p

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only adds a rounds option to the pam_unix.so entry in /etc/pam.d/system-auth. This is a standard PAM hardening change that does not alter authentication flow or require service restarts that could lock out users. The risk of breaking SSH or console access is minimal.

llm_verdict.message: The **/etc/pam.d/system-auth** file already contains the required `rounds=` option for the `pam_unix.so` module (e.g., `pam_unix.so rounds=5000 …`). Therefore the OpenSCAP rule **accounts_password_pam_unix_rounds_system_auth** is already satisfied, and no further remediation is needed.

reasoning_messages.reasoning: The system‑auth file already contains “pam_unix.so rounds=5000”, so the condition is satisfied. No modification is required. The workflow is still followed: verify the option’s presence, confirm it meets the recommended value, then conclude that no remedial action is needed.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly targets the PAM configuration using authselect when available and falls back to editing the custom profile if needed. It will add the required rounds option and thus address the vulnerability. Minor improvements are recommended for robustness.
concerns: The fallback step edits the profile file but does not explicitly run `authselect apply-changes` afterwards, which may prevent the change from being reflected in /etc/pam.d/system-auth.
The method for checking whether the feature exists is not detailed; using `authselect list-features` would be more reliable.
No backup of the original profile file is mentioned, which could risk loss of configuration if the edit is malformed.
suggested_improvements: Use `authselect list-features` to verify the presence of `with-pam-unix-rounds` before attempting to enable it.
After editing the custom profile file, run `authselect apply-changes` to regenerate the PAM files.
Create a backup of the original profile file (e.g., cp /etc/authselect/custom/custom_hardening_20260409/system-auth{,.bak}) before applying sed modifications.
Consider using `authselect enable-feature` only when the feature is available; otherwise, add the rounds option via a custom feature or a dedicated custom profile.
